title: Raghav Chadha sidelined? AAP's Rajya Sabha shuffle triggers rumours; what went wrong
content: Raghav Chadha has been removed as the Aam Aadmi Party's deputy leader in the Rajya Sabha. Ashok Mittal has been appointed in his place. The party has also requested the Rajya Sabha secretariat not to allot speaking time to Chadha from its quota. This move comes amid reports of indiscipline and not adhering to the party line.

title: 'Contradicting himself every day': Macron mocks Trump, rejects US military plan for Hormuz
content: French president Macron criticized Donald Trump's inconsistent Iran policy, deeming military action to reopen the Strait of Hormuz unrealistic. Macron emphasized the need for de-escalation and renewed negotiations. Trump's shifting stances on allies, escalation, and war objectives have drawn international and domestic criticism, with many nations hesitant to join US efforts.

title: 'Publicly berating captains”: Lalit Modi’s jibe at Goenka after LSG match
content: IPL founder Lalit Modi criticized franchise owners for public displays of anger after losses, contrasting them with football league professionalism. He questioned their focus on hype over long-term vision, infrastructure, and fan experience, urging a more patient and professional approach to enhance the league's value.

title: When will Artemis 2 reach the Moon? All you need to know about historic mission
content: In a monumental leap for space exploration, NASA's Artemis II mission is set to reignite humanity's lunar legacy, sending a crew of four astronauts on a groundbreaking 10-day odyssey to the Moon.

title: Oracle layoffs: 6 AM email that laid off 10,000+ employees did not come from HR
content: Oracle has begun its largest-ever layoffs, impacting thousands globally without prior notice. Employees received termination emails early Tuesday, with immediate system access revocation.  The company's aggressive AI data center expansion is cited as a key driver for these cuts, which could affect up to 30,000 staff. Severance packages require signing documents first.

title: ISL: AIFF granted Mohun Bagan, Kerala 3-day extension to pay participation fee
content: The All India Football Federation (AIFF) has granted Mohun Bagan Super Giant and Kerala Blasters an extension until April 5 to pay their first ISL participation fee. Failure to remit the full amount, including daily fines, could lead to disqualification from the next ISL season.

title: Amazon cloud operations in Bahrain reportedly damaged in Iran strike
content: Amazon's cloud operations in Bahrain were reportedly damaged following an Iranian strike, with civil defense forces extinguishing a fire at a company facility. This incident follows threats from Iran's Revolutionary Guards against US companies in the Middle East. AWS facilities in the region have faced disruptions amid the ongoing conflict.

title: Maldives, Sri Lanka others turn to India for energy security amid crisis
content: Amidst escalating Middle East conflict, India is providing petroleum supplies to neighboring nations like Bangladesh, Sri Lanka, Nepal, and Bhutan. The Maldives has also requested petroleum products, with India examining these requests based on its own availability.

title: 'Bengal welcomes tourists': TMC taunts Shah with fish, meat menu ahead of 15-day visit
content: The Trinamool Congress has sharpened its attack on Union Home Minister Amit Shah ahead of his visit to West Bengal, recommending local non-vegetarian delicacies. This move aligns with the TMC's campaign, which portrays the BJP as out of touch with Bengal's cultural traditions, particularly regarding food habits.

title: How an engineer ensured Anthropic's Claude Code source code leak stays on GitHub
content: Anthropic's AI coding assistant's source code leaked, prompting swift copyright takedowns on GitHub. However, one engineer ingeniously bypassed these measures by using AI tools to rewrite the code in Python. This novel approach, taking mere hours, has resulted in a functional, distinct version that evades copyright claims, highlighting challenges in protecting AI code.

title: 'Are we Mossad agents in Iran?': World No. 2 USA Nakamura blasts FIDE
content: World No. 2 American grandmaster Hikaru Nakamura criticised FIDE’s anti-cheating measures at the 2026 Candidates in Cyprus, calling them “complete nonsense.” He joked, “Are we like Mossad agents inside Iran or something?” Nakamura hasn’t raised concerns with FIDE, saying the fears are overblown. After four rounds, he has 1.5 points, sharing results with Anish Giri, Andrey Esipenko, and Wei Yi.

title: Dallas Cowboys and Kansas City Chiefs linked in bold first-round draft shake-up
content: The Dallas Cowboys are considering a proposed draft trade with the Kansas City Chiefs that would move them from pick No. 20 to No. 29 while gaining extra mid-round selections. The idea boosts depth, especially on defense, but risks missing elite talent. Dallas must decide between immediate impact players or building roster depth.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' BO day 15: Ranveer Singh film eyes Rs 950 crore India net
content: 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge' has made box office history, officially crossing the massive Rs. 900 crore India net milestone in just 14 days. The Aditya Dhar directorial, starring Ranveer Singh as the lethal spy Hamza, has collected a staggering Rs 922.26 crore domestic net so far. Despite the ongoing IPL 2026 season, the film continues to draw huge crowds, even outperforming the lifetime collections of the first 'Dhurandhar' in just 11 days. With a global haul of Rs' 1,466 crore, this action epic featuring Sanjay Dutt and Arjun Rampal is now rapidly chasing the elite Rs' 1,000 crore India club.

title: Celtics vs Heat injury update: Who's playing, injured, team stats, and more
content: The Boston Celtics, leading their division, play the Miami Heat at Kaseya Center. Miami needs this win to secure a play-in spot. Both teams have injury concerns. Boston has dominated the season series. The Celtics are favored to win, but Miami's home advantage and determination could make it a close contest.

title: AAP removes Raghav Chadha as party's deputy leader in Rajya Sabha
content: The Aam Aadmi Party has requested the Rajya Sabha Secretariat to remove Raghav Chadha as Deputy Leader of the party in the Upper House.  The party also wants him excluded from speaking time allocated to AAP, proposing Ashok Mittal for the Deputy Leader role. This move indicates a significant internal leadership adjustment in Parliament, though official reasons remain undisclosed.

title: IPL: After snub, RCB star heads to court to secure playing rights
content: Sri Lankan fast bowler Nuwan Thushara has taken Sri Lanka Cricket (SLC) to court, seeking a No Objection Certificate (NOC) for the 2026 Indian Premier League. Thushara argues that SLC's refusal, citing fitness standards, is unreasonable as his contract ended and he informed them of his departure from international cricket.

title: Kylian Mbappe reacts as LEGO unveils Cristiano Ronaldo, Lionel Messi World Cup 2026 set
content: LEGO has launched a new football-themed collection. The collaboration has been launched to celebrate the upcoming FIFA World Cup 2026. The collection features the top names in football. It includes Cristiano Ronaldo, Lionel Messi, Kylian Mbappé, and Vinícius Júnior. Each player has been turned into a LEGO minifigure, giving fans a fun and creative way to celebrate their favorite stars.

title: Oil prices soar, crypto sinks, stock markets crash: Market reaction to Trump speech on Iran
content: US President Trump's conflicting statements on the Iran conflict, suggesting both military success and potential escalation, have triggered global market volatility. Indian stocks initially plunged but recovered, while oil prices surged, impacting nations like Nepal and Bhutan. Precious metals and cryptocurrencies also saw significant declines amid heightened uncertainty.

title: Candidates Chess: How risk-averse Indians self-sabotaged in Round 4
content: India's chess contingent faced a disappointing Round 4 at the Candidates tournament, with R Praggnanandhaa and R Vaishali securing draws while Divya Deshmukh suffered a significant loss. Praggnanandhaa's game was described as uncharacteristically subdued, and Vaishali narrowly escaped a difficult position. Deshmukh's experimental play led to fundamental errors and a decisive defeat.

title: Judicial officers engaged in SIR gheraoed for hours: How Malda 'hostage' drama unfolded
content: The Supreme Court has taken serious note of the Malda hostage incident, issuing show-cause notices to West Bengal's chief secretary and DGP. Protesters, angered by voter list removals, gheraoed judicial officers for hours. The court termed the event a "deplorable" and "calculated" attempt to disrupt the electoral process, demanding central forces for officer safety.

title: KVS admission: Last date today for Class 1, check Balvatika application link
content: KVS admission 2026: Kendriya Vidyalaya Sangathan has set April 02, 2026 as the last date for online applications to Class 1 and Balvatika for the 2026-27 session. Admissions began on March 20, 2026 and follow an online process with lottery-based selection. Merit lists will be released in April, while offline admissions for higher classes depend on vacancies. Eligibility is based on age as of March 31, 2026, with reservation norms and strict document verification applicable.

title: ‘Is Rishabh Pant really an opener?’ Kevin Pietersen questions LSG call
content: Kevin Pietersen questions Rishabh Pant's decision to bat higher up the order, suggesting it could create undue pressure.  Pietersen believes Pant might be pushing himself too far ahead in the batting lineup.  Meanwhile, he lauded young batter Sameer Rizvi for his match-winning performance, calling him a 'proper player' worthy of investment.

title: India’s defence exports 'skyrocket' to all-time high of Rs 38,424 crore; up 62%
content: India's defence exports reached a record Rs 38,424 crore in FY 2025-26, a significant 62.66% surge from the previous fiscal year. This achievement, driven by both public sector undertakings and private firms, highlights growing global confidence in India's indigenous defence manufacturing capabilities and its ambition to become a global hub for defence equipment.

title: Gold futures sink nearly 4% after Trump remarks on Iran; check rates in your city
content: Gold prices plummeted by Rs 6,004 to Rs 1,47,704 per 10 grams on Thursday, reversing a four-day rally. Intensifying West Asian geopolitical tensions and a strengthened US dollar, following President Trump's warnings of further action against Iran, dampened investor sentiment. This shift also saw traders fully price out US Federal Reserve interest rate cuts for 2026.

title: India scraps import tax on petrochemicals: Govt may lose over $190 mn in revenue
content: India has waived customs duties on 40 petrochemical products until June 2026, costing an estimated 18 billion rupees. This emergency measure aims to counter supply disruptions from the Middle East conflict, stabilize prices, and support key industries like plastics, pharmaceuticals, and textiles by ensuring raw material availability and reducing production costs.

title: Priyadarshan & Akshay's 'Bhooth Bangla' pushed to April 16 amid 'Dhurandhar 2' BO run
content: Prepare for a spine-tingling laugh riot with 'Bhooth Bangla'! Starring the dynamic duo of Akshay Kumar and Wamiqa Gabbi, this horror-comedy is set to creep onto screens with paid previews kicking off on April 16, 2026, at 9 PM. Following the action-packed 'Dhurandhar: The Revenge', this release is a calculated strategy to dominate box office space.

title: Divya Dutta says Amitabh Bachchan felt she wasn’t nice to him on ‘Baghban’ set
content: For Divya Dutta, working with Amitabh Bachchan early in her career turned into a life lesson she hasn’t forgotten. Speaking about her experience on the sets of Baghban, the actor recalled how she struggled to separate her on-screen character from real life.

title: ‘Gym se body ban jayegi, bowling nahi’: Shami’s one line that sparked Mohsin Khan’s comeback
content: Mohsin Khan, 27, whose career has been marred by injuries, made his return to competitive cricket after a gap of 455 days. Since bursting onto the scene in IPL 2022, Mohsin has survived a career-threatening shoulder injury, a groin injury, and then a torn anterior cruciate ligament (ACL). On Wednesday, he showed a glimpse of his best in his opening spell against Delhi Capitals, which read 3-1-6-1.

title: KKR vs SRH, IPL 2026: 5 players to watch in today’s match
content: Kolkata Knight Riders face Sunrisers Hyderabad with their spinners needing to find form at Eden Gardens.  KKR's bowling depth is tested by injuries, while SRH's bowling faces scrutiny.  Key players like Abhishek Sharma and Varun Chakravarthy will be crucial in this high-stakes encounter.

title: Who is Ashok Mittal? MP who replaces Raghav Chadha as AAP's deputy leader
content: The Aam Aadmi Party has requested the Rajya Sabha secretariat to remove Raghav Chadha as the party's deputy leader in the House and cease allocating him speaking time from AAP's quota. Sources indicate that Rajya Sabha MP Ashok Kumar Mittal, founder of Lovely Professional University, is being considered as his replacement.

title: 'Minutes away from striking Pak from sea': Navy chief reveals key Op Sindoor moment
content: Indian Navy Chief Admiral Dinesh K Tripathi revealed the Navy was moments from striking Pakistan by sea during Operation Sindoor. Pakistan requested a halt to military actions. The operation, launched after the Pahalgam terror attack, targeted terrorist bases. The Navy's swift deployment and aggressive posture demonstrated its capabilities. Nine major terrorist camps were identified and struck with precision.

title: Parliament Budget Session LIVE: RS passes Jan Vishwas Bill; LS discusses CAPF Bill
content: Parliament passed Andhra Pradesh Reorganisation (Amendment) Bill, 2026 to recognise Amaravati as permanent capital of State

title: Minister urges entrepreneurs to set up industries in Raichur
content: Boseraju was speaking after inaugurating Tejas workshop at the Jagajyoti Basaveshwara Auditorium on UAS campus in Raichur

title: Nandyal MP seeks UNESCO’s Intangible Cultural Heritage tag for Ahobilam Paruveta Utsavam
content: Tribals of 33 villages in Nandyal district have been celebrating Paruvata Utsavam for 40 days every year for the past several decades, says Shabari

title: Govt employees mandated to complete competency-linked courses: Centre in Rajya Sabha
content: Union Minister of State for Personnel says the Government has adopted a competency-based capacity building approach, focusing on role-based learning and continuous skill development

title: Ashok Kharat case: Godman impregnated victim, gave her abortion pills, say police
content: The prosecution said Ashok Kharat sexually exploited the victim several times between February 2020 and March 2026, and took away Aadhaar and PAN cards of her relatives

title: Conflict in West Asia hits foundries of Shivamogga, industries fear temporary closure
content: Facing the shortage in the supply of raw materials, along with rising logistics costs, industrialists fear they may have to halt production if the conflict continues for a few more weeks.

title: India slams attacks on UNIFIL soldiers, does not name Israel
content: “As one of the largest and longest serving contributors to Peacekeeping, and in consonance with UNSC resolution 2589, we seek accountability for crimes against peacekeepers,” said the MEA spokesperson

title: HRD Minister Lokesh thanks Prime Minister Modi for ensuring passage of Amaravati Bill in Parliament
content: It reaffirms confidence in Amaravati as the State’s sole capital and is a pointer to the strong coordination between the State and the Centre, he says

title: CPI(M) slams AIR, Doordarshan in Assam for censorship of its speech
content: Ahead of Assembly polls, the party said its criticisms of the anti-people policies of the BJP-led government in Assam were deleted

title: How Vizag Astronomy Club is bringing stargazing back to Visakhapatnam
content: Vizag Astronomy Club’s moon watch sessions and terrace viewings are drawing residents back to the night sky

title: New Income Tax Act 2025: Breaking down benefits for individuals
content: As per the Income Tax Act 2025, which comes into effect from April 1, 2026, Forms 15G and 15H merged into a single unified Form 121; Form 26AS renamed as Form 168 and much more...

title: Forces will take ‘unprecedented action’ if Pakistan attempts any misadventure, warns Rajnath
content: Defence Minister Rajnath Singh said the armed forces brought Pakistan to its knees in 22 minutes during Operation Sindoor, he said the military operation is still on

title: Assembly Election LIVE: Rahul Gandhi releases Congress manifesto for Assam polls, with focus on 11 sectors
content: The 11 resolutions spread across areas like governance, identity, healthcare, infrastructure development, industrialisation, agriculture, rural and urban development, climate change and secured Assam.

title: Governor bound by Cabinet’s advice: Madras High Court
content: Justices A.D. Jagadish Chandira, G.K. Ilanthiraiyan and Sunder Mohan answer a reference made to them by a Division Bench of the High Court

title: Over 20 Indian workers died abroad daily in last 5 years, most in Gulf nations: govt data
content: Govt data showed that the highest number of deaths occurred in 2021, with 8,234 Indian workers losing their lives abroad; Gulf countries accounted for over 86% of these deaths

title: Government confirms extra sittings to debate amendments to Women’s Reservation Act 2023
content: Congress accuses government of maintaining unnecessary secrecy and alleges that the amendments are being brought in the middle of the election campaign eyeing possible electoral benefits

title: Rayagada’s Nagavali riverfront offers kayaking, village trails and a quiet escape
content: A short drive from Visakhapatnam leads to Anija in Rayagada, southern Odisha, where kayaking, golf and nature trails unfold along the Nagavali River

title: CAG flags ₹3.69 lakh crore cess transfer shortfall; RJD MP demands accountability in Rajya Sabha
content: CAG’s findings reveal decades-long discrepancies in cess fund transfers, with RJD MP A.D. Singh demanding clarity on the government’s financial accountability

title: Empower, don't just accommodate autistic persons: Rajya Sabha Chairman
content: Rajya Sabha Chairman C.P. Radhakrishnan reaffirmed Parliament's commitment to safeguarding the rights and promoting the welfare of persons with autism

title: Himanta 'most corrupt CM' in India; Modi, Shah, Sarma running 'land ATM' in Assam: Rahul Gandhi
content: India's most corrupt CM is Himanta Biswa Sarma, and his family is also No 1 in corruption. The Congress government will take action against him. Though he is boasting himself now, he will be completely silent after that, says Congress leader Rahul Gandhi

title: Shortcut to poribortan in Bengal passes through Bhabanipur, says Amit Shah
content: Top BJP leader and Union Home Minister Amit Shah turned the filing of nomination papers of Mr. Adhikari and three other BJP candidates into a high-voltage political spectacle by accompanying the BJP candidates on a roadshow

title: Speaker Birla cautions ministers, members against long chats in House chamber
content: As soon as the Zero Hour began at 12 noon, Om Birla said he has seen members and ministers talking for half-an-hour with each other during House proceedings

title: AAP writes to Rajya Sabha Secretariat to remove Raghav Chadha as deputy leader
content: The move comes amid Mr. Chadha’s prolonged silence on matters related to the AAP, and his absence from several public events led by AAP supremo Arvind Kejriwal

title: Amaravati is people's sentiment, Jagan’s hatred for it will not change: Andhra CM Chandrababu Naidu
content: Andhra CM Chandrababu Naidu said the feeling that “Amaravati is our capital” should resonate with everyone

title: Nitish Kumar to get Z-Plus security
content: As the Bihar CM, Nitish Kumar enjoyed a special security cover provided under the State’s Bihar Special Security Act, 2000, which is modelled after the SPG Act meant for the Prime Minister

title: Kerala Assembly polls: IUML rocked by Noorbina Rasheed’s resignation from Vanitha League
content: Her resignation as national general secretary of Vanitha League, the party’s women’s wing, follows reports of internal discontent over representation of women on IUML’s candidate list for Assembly elections

title: Kerala Assembly polls: UDF candidate in Kanhangad faces complaint for alleged religious appeal for votes
content: According to the complaint, the incident occurred on March 22 when the candidate visited a church. It is alleged that while canvassing for votes, he made references to religion and community, in violation of Model Code of Conduct

title: Mumbai BMC’s Standing Committee approves Gargai project
content: The Gargai project has been long overdue and was reinitiated by the Mahayuti government in 2019 to build a research project to procure 440 MLD for Mumbai

title: 2.8 kg of MDMA seized in major drug bust in Mukkam in Kerala’s Kozhikode
content: In an operation by the District Anti-Narcotics Special Action Force, 36-year-old Mohammed Haneefa, suspected to be a key wholesale supplier, was arrested

title: Chhattisgarh High Court convicts Amit Jogi in 2003 NCP leader murder case
content: The case pertains to the killing of businessman-politician Ramavatar Jaggi in Raipur at a time when Ajit Jogi was the sitting Chief Minister of the State.

title: Census 2027: Frequently asked questions on the self-enumeration process
content: Here are some frequently asked questions (FAQs) related to Census 2027 and the self enumeration process.

title: PM Modi visit on April 3: Red zones declared in parts of Chennai; drone ban enforced
content: Officials said that the restrictions will be in force for two days, April 3 and 4, during which flying of drone cameras and other unmanned aerial objects will be strictly prohibited in the designated areas

title: Kerala Assembly elections: Prakash Karat says ‘nothing wrong’ in SDPI backing LDF as tactical move
content: Senior CPI(M) leader says CPI(M)’s ‘policy is clear in not having any understanding with organisations that are communal or extremist in character’ and that there is ‘no personality cult’ in the party

title: Per capita spending varies by 2.5 times across Bengaluru corporations: Report
content: The per capita outlay refers to the amount a corporation is spending on an individual in its jurisdiction

title: GBA budget 2026: How Bengaluru’s new corporations differ in what they earn and spend
content: Property tax contributes 51% of its own source revenue in East and 42% in Central, but drops to around 34–37% in North, West and South

title: Tamil Nadu Assembly election 2026: What is the share of seats for parties in AIADMK-led alliance?
content: Of the 234 Assembly constituencies in the State, the AIADMK will contest in 167 constituencies, while the remaining seats have been allotted to its alliance partners

title: GBA Budget 2026 explained: How Bengaluru’s new corporations differ in what they earn and spend
content: On March 27 and 28, the Bengaluru Corporations presented their respective budgets with a total outlay of ₹20,217 crore.

title: Mysuru railway division registers record passenger revenue, strong commercial growth
content: The division registered its highest-ever passenger revenue of ₹449.13 crore, surpassing the previous record of ₹419.89 crore

title: Kerala polls: Congress-led UDF manifesto promises ‘Indira guarantees’, five ‘dream’ projects and boost for health, higher education sectors
content: Five guarantees include free bus travel for all women in KSRTC buses, ₹1,000 monthly financial aid for college-going girl students, increase in welfare pension to ₹3,000 per month, ₹25-lakh health insurance cover for every household and interest-free loans up to ₹5 lakh for the youth to start own business

title: ‘Patriot’ postponed: Mammootty-Mohanlal movie gets new release date
content: The highly anticipated Malayalam film ‘Patriot’, featuring the legendary duo Mohanlal and Mammootty, has pushed its release date thanks to some “unforeseen circumstances,”

title: FIR against Rajasthan MLA Ravindra Singh Bhati for threatening folk singer Rawna
content: Folk singer Chotu Singh Rawna lodged the FIR against the Sheo MLA and five others after a controversy erupted over a comment on social media

title: Previous AIADMK govt. failed to act effectively on crimes against women, alleges Tamil Nadu CM Stalin
content: He said that during the DMK rule, in 86% of cases involving crimes against women and children, chargesheets were filed within 60 days

title: Kerala Assembly polls: LDF’s manifesto vows to eradicate absolute poverty, hike welfare pensions to ₹3,000
content: Ruling front’s manifesto outlines a 60-point programme containing 950 proposals to create a ‘Nava Kerala’ (New Kerala), while seeking a mandate to continue ongoing development works

title: Secretive jungle cats need habitats outside protected areas: study
content: A new study’s finding that jungle cats use agricultural landscapes aligns with previous knowledge of the species. In and around farms, these cats keep rodent populations in check, thus ‘protecting’ crops. However, these landscapes lie outside protected areas and harbour several threats, including fragmented habitats and speeding vehicles

title: Traffic diverted from old bridge to new bridge across Kabini river on Mysuru-Nanjangud stretch of NH 766
content: National Highways Authority of India (NHAI) sought the diversion of all vehicular traffic from the old bridge to the newly constructed bridge citing the need for repair and reconstruction of the old bridge, which is in a deteriorated condition

title: Suvendu Adhikari files nomination from Nandigram, says BJP will register decisive victory in Bengal
content: The BJP has fielded him from the two seats in a high-profile electoral battle against the ruling Trinamool Congress

title: Uttar Pradesh top performer under PM’s solar power scheme: UPNEDA
content: As per the national portal, Uttar Pradesh has achieved 4,48,233 cumulative rooftop solar installations, placing it third in India

content: Arjun Sampath of Indu Makkal Katchi also claims to have reliably learnt that 90% of nominations filed so far in the 44 reserved constituencies in Tamil Nadu are by Christians

content: Expressing concern over financial management, Ramji Lal Suman said the temple generates significant revenue but raised questions about the administration of its funds.

content: After putting up 41 points and 18 rebounds in a runaway win, Spurs center Victor Wembanyama reiterated that the MVP award is squarely in his sights.

title: Rams receiver Nacua is in rehab, attorney says
content: Rams wide receiver Puka Nacua has checked in to rehab, his attorney told The California Post. Last month, a woman in Los Angeles filed a lawsuit against Nacua alleging he made an "unprovoked antisemitic statement" and later bit her on the shoulder.

title: Woods can leave U.S. for treatment, judge rules
content: A Florida judge granted Tiger Woods' request on Wednesday to travel outside the U.S. and enter a comprehensive inpatient treatment facility.

title: Kansas coach Self to return for 2026-27 season
content: Kansas men's basketball coach Bill Self is set to return for the 2026-27 season, saying in a statement Wednesday that he remains "focused and committed to Kansas" and "competing for a National Championship."

title: QB Mendoza puts on show at Indiana pro day
content: Expected No. 1 pick Fernando Mendoza threw roughly 56 passes in front of scouts from all 32 NFL teams and more than 100 members of the media Wednesday.

title: NCAA proposes ban on players who stay in draft
content: The NCAA Division I Cabinet will consider proposed changes to eligibility rules, including one that would bar athletes who have entered and remained in a professional sports draft from competing in college.

title: Sources: Rapper J. Cole to play pro hoops in China
content: Rap megastar J. Cole has signed a contract to play in the Chinese Basketball Association with the Nanjing Monkey Kings, sources told ESPN on Wednesday.

title: Ranking all 48 teams at the World Cup

title: WC final tickets hit $10,990 as FIFA reopens sales
content: the top ticket price for the world cup final was $10,990 . the price had been $8,680 when FIFA sold tickets after the tournament draw in December . tickets were listed for 17 of the 72 group stage matches by Wednesday night .

title: Women's March Madness: The 15 best players left in the field
content: the final women's college basketball player rankings for 2025-26 are pared down to 15 as the tournament shifts to Phoenix for the national semifinals . Sarah Strong has been no. 1 since the preseason rankings . she is trying to replicate what former Huskies great Breanna Stewart did: win four national championships in four seasons .
